items.headline: U.S. Futures Dip as Oil Prices and AI Stock Swings Dominate Sentiment
items.summary: U.S. stock futures edged lower on Thursday, July 23, 2026, as investors continued to assess a fresh wave of technology earnings and escalating geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, which pushed Brent crude past $97 a barrel. The previous day, Wall Street saw major indices mixed, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq closing lower, while the Dow remained largely flat, amidst ongoing volatility in AI-related stocks.

items.headline: Hyliion Surges on U.S. Navy Contract
items.summary: Hyliion ($HYLN) shares jumped 15% in pre-market trading after securing a $41.7 million U.S. Navy contract for KARNO power generation systems, signaling significant government validation for its technology.

items.headline: Supermicro Rises on Strong Outlook
items.summary: Super Micro Computer ($SMCI) climbed 20.2% after hours on Wednesday, driven by an improved profitability outlook that bolstered investor confidence in AI server stocks.

items.headline: United Rentals Beats Estimates
items.summary: United Rentals ($URI) shares gained 8% in pre-market trading after exceeding Q2 revenue and earnings estimates and raising its full-year guidance, attributed to robust construction and industrial project demand.

items.headline: Boxabl Declines
items.summary: Boxabl ($BXBL) saw a sharp decline of 23.85% in pre-market trading.

items.headline: GE Vernova Falls on Earnings Reaction
items.summary: GE Vernova ($GEV) dropped 8.69% in pre-market trading, as investors reacted to its recent earnings report and details regarding a buyback tranche.

items.headline: Tesla Shares Drop on Earnings Miss
items.summary: Tesla's ($TSLA) share price fell 1.3% on Wednesday and an additional 4.1% in after-hours trading, following the company reporting adjusted earnings per share of $0.33, missing analyst expectations.

items.headline: Tech Sector Shows Mixed Performance Amid AI Focus
items.summary: The technology sector experienced mixed performance on Wednesday, July 22, 2026, as investors focused on key earnings reports to gauge the health of the artificial intelligence-driven market rally. Semiconductor stocks, after initially struggling, showed some recovery, while software stocks generally weakened.

items.headline: Alphabet Beats Q2 Estimates, Shares Dip After Hours
items.summary: Alphabet Inc. ($GOOGL), Google's parent company, reported stronger-than-expected second-quarter results after Wednesday's close, indicating its significant artificial intelligence investments are yielding returns. However, its share price fell 2.9% in after-hours trading despite the positive earnings.

items.headline: Oil Prices Surge Amid Mideast Tensions
items.summary: Brent crude oil prices surged past $97 a barrel on Thursday, July 23, 2026, due to intensifying geopolitical tensions in the Middle East. This rise in oil prices is contributing to inflationary risks and impacting global markets.

items.headline: U.S. Economic Data Reflects Higher Borrowing Costs and Slower Job Growth
items.summary: U.S. stock futures are under pressure as investors consider the implications of higher borrowing costs, with the US 10-year Treasury yield near 4.63%, and a cooling jobs market indicated by ADP private hiring at approximately 16,500 jobs per week.

items.headline: Japan Unveils Economic Growth Strategy for H2 2026
items.summary: Japan's Ministry of Finance and Economy revealed its Economic Growth Strategy for H2 2026, focusing on 'Three Mega Projects': Semiconductors, AI Data Centers (AIDCs), and Physical AI, alongside plans to double memory production capacity.
